why: The read
Broke the $7.85 support I was watching and kept bleeding to $6.92 — the bounce never came. Washed out down here, but no buyer showing up yet.
Watch for
A real base + reclaim of ~$7.50 on volume before any call. Not yet — don't catch it.
The read
Puts have been THE winner here ($8 → $6.92). But the easy down-money already happened — at $6.92 it's oversold and due a bounce. Buying puts now = catching the knife on the short side.
Watch for
A failed bounce / rejection at ~$7.85 is a cleaner short entry than chasing fresh lows. Shorting down here is late.
The read
Faded from the highs; the $30 break never came. Drifting in the upper-mid of its range — no momentum either way.
Watch for
A clean break of $30 for a call. Until then, no setup.
The read
Too two-sided to short — it's holding the upper-mid range, not breaking down. No clean put edge.
Watch for
A break of ~$22 support would open a put case. Not there.
The read
Dead-cat bounce fading, stuck mid-range, catalyst spent.
Watch for
Nothing for calls — $11.75 resistance overhead, nothing new until Sept 9.
The read
The mildest SHORT lean on your list — a faded bounce rolling back over in a long downtrend. But a lot's already fallen ($30 → $10), so it's not pristine.
Watch for
A break of ~$8.65 support = the put trigger. Above it, no rush — let it break first.
The read
Small bounce off the floor, but still −25% on the month with the earnings damage unresolved — and the options trade thin.
Watch for
A real base, not a dead-cat pop. Thin options make either side expensive to trade.
The read
Just bounced — puts would be fighting the turn now, and the options are too thin (~1M shares/day) to short cleanly anyway.
Watch for
If it rolls back over and loses $9.81 on volume, maybe — but wide spreads make it rough either way.
The read
The one name showing life — reclaimed the $50 floor and bounced to $53. But a call here runs $300+, well out of your $100 budget.
Watch for
Track the bounce; trade it for real when the account's bigger.
The read
Just turned UP off $50 — puts are the wrong side now. The breakdown already played out and reversed.
Watch for
Nothing short here; it's recovering, not breaking down.
